sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is essential for all stages of SCLC. For limited-stage disease, it is combined with thoracic RT for definitive treatment. For extensive-stage disease, it is the primary treatment, often combined with immunotherapy. The standard induction is four cycles of platinum/etoposide-based chemotherapy. Immunotherapy with atezolizumab or durvalumab is now a standard first-line component for extensive-stage disease and as consolidation for limited-stage disease [1].

cards.name: Cisplatin/Etoposide (Limited-stage)
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for limited-stage SCLC with concurrent RT. Drugs: Cisplatin 75 mg/m² IV Day 1; Etoposide 100 mg/m² IV Days 1, 2, 3. Cycle days: 21 days. Key toxicities: Myelosuppression, nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, neurotoxicity, nausea/vomiting [1].

cards.name: Cisplatin/Etoposide (Limited-stage, alternative dosing)
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for limited-stage SCLC with concurrent RT. Drugs: Cisplatin 60 mg/m² IV Day 1; Etoposide 120 mg/m² IV Days 1, 2, 3. Cycle days: 21 days. Key toxicities: Similar to above [1].

cards.name: Carboplatin/Etoposide (Limited-stage)
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for limited-stage SCLC with concurrent RT. Drugs: Carboplatin AUC 5–6 IV Day 1; Etoposide 100 mg/m² IV Days 1, 2, 3. Cycle days: 21–28 days. Key toxicities: Myelosuppression (greater risk than cisplatin), nausea/vomiting [1].

cards.name: Cisplatin/Etoposide (Limited-stage, other recommended)
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for limited-stage SCLC with concurrent RT. Drugs: Cisplatin 25 mg/m² IV Days 1, 2, 3; Etoposide 100 mg/m² IV Days 1, 2, 3. Cycle days: 21 days. Key toxicities: Similar to above [1].

cards.name: Carboplatin/Etoposide + Atezolizumab (Extensive-stage, induction)
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for extensive-stage SCLC. Drugs: Carboplatin AUC 5 IV Day 1; Etoposide 100 mg/m² IV Days 1, 2, 3; Atezolizumab 1200 mg IV Day 1. Cycle days: 21 days for 4 cycles. Key toxicities: Myelosuppression, immune-mediated adverse events (e.g., pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis) [1, 118, 120].

cards.name: Atezolizumab maintenance (Extensive-stage)
cards.detail: Setting: Maintenance after induction chemoimmunotherapy for extensive-stage SCLC. Drugs: Atezolizumab 1200 mg IV Day 1. Cycle days: 28 days. Key toxicities: Immune-mediated adverse events [1].

cards.name: Durvalumab consolidation (Limited-stage)
cards.detail: Setting: Consolidation therapy after chemoradiation for limited-stage SCLC. Drugs: Durvalumab 1500 mg IV Day 1 every 28 days for up to 24 months. Key toxicities: Immune-mediated adverse events. Rationale: ADRIATIC trial demonstrated significant OS and PFS improvement [1, 115].

timeline.title: 1st Line (Limited-stage)
timeline.body: Cisplatin/Etoposide or Carboplatin/Etoposide with concurrent RT (category 1 for good PS 0–2). Alternatives: Cisplatin 25 mg/m² Days 1,2,3 + Etoposide 100 mg/m² Days 1,2,3.

timeline.title: 1st Line (Extensive-stage)
timeline.body: Platinum/etoposide + atezolizumab (category 1) or platinum/etoposide + durvalumab (category 1). Alternatives: Platinum/etoposide alone, irinotecan-based regimens.

timeline.title: Consolidation (Limited-stage)
timeline.body: Durvalumab 1500 mg every 28 days for up to 24 months (category 1). Alternatives: None listed.

timeline.title: Subsequent Therapy (Relapsed/Refractory)
timeline.body: Tarlatamab-dlle (category 1), irinotecan, lurbinectedin (if not previously used), platinum rechallenge for sensitive relapse, topotecan. Alternatives: Nivolumab, pembrolizumab, CAV, docetaxel, gemcitabine, oral etoposide, paclitaxel, temozolomide.
